questions.q: In which city is Dashashwamedh Ghat located?
questions.explanation: Dashashwamedh Ghat is the most famous riverfront ghat in Varanasi (Kashi), Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: On the bank of which river does Dashashwamedh Ghat lie?
questions.explanation: The ghat is a flight of steps leading down to the sacred river Ganga at Varanasi.

questions.q: Dashashwamedh Ghat is most famous for which daily ceremony performed each evening?
questions.explanation: The grand evening Ganga Aarti, a fire-and-lamp ritual to the river goddess, is performed here daily before crowds of pilgrims.

questions.q: In which Indian state is Dashashwamedh Ghat located?
questions.explanation: Varanasi, home to Dashashwamedh Ghat, is in the state of Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: What is a 'ghat' such as Dashashwamedh Ghat?
questions.explanation: A ghat is a series of steps leading down to a body of water, used for bathing and rituals.

questions.q: The Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat is primarily a worship offered to whom?
questions.explanation: The aarti is a devotional offering of light and fire to the river goddess Ganga.

questions.q: Varanasi, where Dashashwamedh Ghat lies, is sacred mainly to followers of which deity?
questions.explanation: Varanasi (Kashi) is one of the holiest cities of Lord Shiva, with the nearby Kashi Vishwanath temple at its heart.

questions.q: At what time of day is the most famous Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat held?
questions.explanation: The grand, choreographed Ganga Aarti for which the ghat is renowned takes place every evening after sunset.

questions.q: What do pilgrims most commonly do in the Ganga at Dashashwamedh Ghat?
questions.explanation: Pilgrims descend the steps to take a holy dip in the Ganga, believed to cleanse sins.

questions.q: Dashashwamedh Ghat is often described as the principal and most central ghat of which riverfront?
questions.explanation: It is regarded as the main and busiest ghat along the long crescent of ghats on the Varanasi waterfront.

questions.q: The name 'Dashashwamedh' refers to which legendary event?
questions.explanation: The name literally means 'ten horse sacrifices' (dasha = ten, ashwamedha = horse sacrifice), said to have been performed here.

questions.q: According to popular legend, which deity is said to have performed or sanctioned the ten horse sacrifices that give the ghat its name?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Lord Brahma created the ghat and performed the Dasha-Ashwamedha sacrifice to welcome Shiva to Kashi.

questions.q: The famous Maratha queen Ahilyabai Holkar is credited with rebuilding and patronizing many Varanasi ghats and temples in which century?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai Holkar of Indore sponsored extensive rebuilding of Varanasi's ghats and temples in the 18th century.

questions.q: Dashashwamedh Ghat is located very close to which famous Shiva temple?
questions.explanation: The ghat sits a short walk from the Kashi Vishwanath Temple, the principal Shiva shrine of Varanasi.

questions.q: During the evening Ganga Aarti, priests typically wave large multi-tiered lamps fueled by what?
questions.explanation: The priests swing tall brass lamps lit with camphor and ghee or oil, accompanied by bells, conch, and chanting.

questions.q: Small floating leaf-cup offerings with a lamp and flowers, set adrift on the Ganga by devotees, are known as what?
questions.explanation: Devotees float lit diyas (leaf-cups with a lamp and flowers) on the river as part of the worship at the ghat.

questions.q: Which ancient name for Varanasi is closely associated with the city around Dashashwamedh Ghat?
questions.explanation: Varanasi has been known since antiquity as Kashi, 'the city of light,' considered among the world's oldest living cities.

questions.q: The organization most associated with conducting the daily grand Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat is a local committee of which group?
questions.explanation: The aarti is organized and performed by a body of local priests (a Ganga Seva or aarti committee) each evening.

questions.q: Visitors commonly view the Dashashwamedh Ghat aarti not only from the steps but also from where?
questions.explanation: Crowds watch the aarti from the ghat steps and from rows of boats anchored on the Ganga facing the ceremony.

questions.q: Roughly how many priests typically perform the synchronized evening aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat?
questions.explanation: A small group of priests, commonly around five to seven, perform the lamps in synchronized fashion on raised platforms.

questions.q: The Varanasi ghats including Dashashwamedh primarily face which compass direction across the Ganga?
options: The river bends so the city bank faces broadly toward the rising sun
Toward the mountains in the north
questions.explanation: At Varanasi the Ganga curves so the ghat-lined city bank faces broadly eastward, letting bathers greet the rising sun over the water.

questions.q: Dashashwamedh Ghat is one of how many primary ghats traditionally counted along the Varanasi waterfront?
questions.explanation: Varanasi's riverfront is lined with dozens of ghats, commonly counted at around eighty or more, of which Dashashwamedh is the most prominent.

questions.q: Which Maratha Peshwa is traditionally credited with constructing or restoring Dashashwamedh Ghat in the 1740s?
questions.explanation: Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao (Nana Saheb) is credited with rebuilding the present ghat in 1748, with later additions by Maratha patrons.

questions.q: An additional reconstruction of Dashashwamedh Ghat in 1774 is associated with which Holkar queen of Indore?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai Holkar, the Holkar queen of Indore, rebuilt the ghat in 1774, consistent with her wide patronage of Varanasi's riverfront in the 18th century.

questions.q: Beyond the Brahma legend, a second tradition links Dashashwamedh Ghat's name to ten horse sacrifices said to be performed by which legendary king?
questions.explanation: A parallel tradition attributes the ten ashwamedha sacrifices to the legendary Kashi king Divodasa.

questions.q: Dashashwamedh Ghat lies on which bank of the Ganga as the river flows past Varanasi?
questions.explanation: All of Varanasi's principal ghats, including Dashashwamedh, line the western bank, where the city stands; the eastern bank is largely sandy and undeveloped.

questions.q: Which two well-known Varanasi ghats lie on either side and frame Dashashwamedh Ghat along the riverfront?
options: Assi Ghat and Adi Keshava Ghat
Man Mandir Ghat and Prayag Ghat
questions.explanation: Dashashwamedh is flanked by neighbors such as Man Mandir Ghat and Prayag Ghat along the central stretch of the Varanasi riverfront.

questions.q: The Man Mandir Ghat observatory near Dashashwamedh Ghat was built by which Rajput ruler famed for his observatories?
options: Sawai Jai Singh II of Jaipur
questions.explanation: The astronomical instruments atop the adjacent Man Mandir Ghat were installed by Sawai Jai Singh II of Jaipur, builder of the Jantar Mantar observatories.

questions.q: Is Varanasi's ghats, including Dashashwamedh, a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
options: No, it is not a UNESCO World Heritage Site
questions.explanation: Despite their antiquity, the Varanasi ghats are not (as of well-established status) inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: The Sheetla (Shitala) Temple, a notable shrine, stands directly at which ghat?
questions.explanation: The Sheetla Devi temple is located right at Dashashwamedh Ghat, drawing devotees alongside the bathing and aarti crowds.
